# UC-13.1.36 · Configuration File Change Tracking (_audit)

## Description

Unauthorized `.conf` edits change data access and ingestion behavior; rapid detection limits blast radius.

## Value

Unauthorized `.conf` edits change data access and ingestion behavior; rapid detection limits blast radius.

## Implementation

Map `action` types to your Splunk version. Alert on changes outside change windows. Route to SecOps for prod SH/CM.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Audit trail.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_audit` (`action` for config changes).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map `action` types to your Splunk version. Alert on changes outside change windows. Route to SecOps for prod SH/CM.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_audit action IN ("edit_*","update")
| search file_path="*.conf" OR object_path="*local*"
| table _time, user, action, file_path, object_path
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Configuration File Change Tracking (_audit)** — Unauthorized `.conf` edits change data access and ingestion behavior; rapid detection limits blast radius.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_audit` (`action` for config changes). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Audit trail. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_audit. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Configuration File Change Tracking (_audit)**): table _time, user, action, file_path, object_path
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over calm and busy windows. Compare with the Splunk monitoring console and relevant health and introspection views, and spot-check key fields in sample events.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (config changes), Table (recent edits by user).

## SPL

```spl
index=_audit action IN ("edit_*","update")
| search file_path="*.conf" OR object_path="*local*"
| table _time, user, action, file_path, object_path
| sort -_time
```
